proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all 122 features, at least some show a statistically significant univariate association with pfs_months (Pearson correlation, t-test, or one-way ANOVA depending on dtype).
feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65) has a positive Pearson correlation with pfs_months.
feature_013 (continuous, wide range 0.12-3622, PSA-like distribution) is negatively correlated with pfs_months on the log scale.

analyses.code: for c in features: pearsonr/ttest/anova(df[c], df['pfs_months']); rank by p
analyses.result_summary: Univariate scan over all 122 features. Strongest signals: feature_078 r=+0.856 (p≈0); feature_057 r=-0.388 (p≈0); log(feature_013) r=-0.231 (p≈0); feature_006 r=-0.143 (p≈0); feature_051 mean diff=-0.521 mo (p=1.6e-181); feature_009 r=+0.123 (p=1.5e-166). Many other features are unassociated.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 has an approximately linear positive relationship with pfs_months: each one-unit increase increases mean pfs_months by ~0.17 months.

analyses.result_summary: Linear regression: slope=+0.1740 months per unit feature_078 (intercept=-7.567), r=0.856, p≈0. Mean pfs_months rises monotonically across deciles of feature_078 from 0.01 mo (age 30-36) to 7.82 mo (age 84-90).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (3-level ordinal: 0/1/2) has a strong negative gradient with pfs_months: higher levels indicate worse prognosis (consistent with metastasis burden).

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_057=0 → 4.68 mo (n=17592); =1 → 3.49 mo (n=24971); =2 → 2.38 mo (n=7437). One-way ANOVA F=4445.9, p≈0. Each pairwise t-test also p<1e-300. Per-level decrement ≈ -1.15 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: log(feature_013) (PSA-like) is negatively associated with pfs_months: each unit increase in log(feature_013) decreases mean pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: log(feature_013) Pearson r=-0.231 (p≈0); univariate slope ≈ -0.5 mo per unit log. Confirms a clinically plausible inverse relation between this PSA-like marker and progression-free survival.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8 — albumin-like) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher values predict longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: feature_009 Pearson r=+0.123 (p=1.5e-166). Mean pfs_months rises across quintiles: 2.72 (1.5-2.3) → 3.30 (2.3-3.1) → 3.64 (3.1-3.9) → 3.97 (3.9-4.7) → 4.37 (4.7-5.5). Gradient consistent with albumin.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (continuous, range 0-24.6, mean 3.84) is negatively associated with pfs_months — higher values indicate worse prognosis.

analyses.result_summary: feature_006 Pearson r=-0.143 (p=9.9e-226). Quintile means decline from 3.92 (0-4.9) to 2.81 (14.8-19.7), partly recovering at the top. 27.5% of patients have feature_006=0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs by self-reported race (feature_018: white/hispanic/black/asian/other).
Specifically, Black patients have shorter mean pfs_months than white patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by race: asian 3.797, black 3.741, hispanic 3.737, other 3.749, white 3.741. ANOVA F=0.56, p=0.689. White vs Black t-test p=0.987; white vs hispanic p=0.886; white vs asian p=0.145. NO racial difference in pfs_months in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs by insurance status (feature_045: private/medicare/medicaid/uninsured).
Specifically, uninsured patients have shorter mean pfs_months than privately insured patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by insurance: medicare 3.754, private 3.736, medicaid 3.741, uninsured 3.726. ANOVA F=0.34, p=0.794. Private vs uninsured t-test p=0.832. No insurance disparity in pfs_months at the marginal level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (range 6-10, Gleason-like) is negatively correlated with pfs_months — higher Gleason predicts shorter pfs_months.
The five 0-4 ordinal features (feature_016, _071, _096, _033, _064 — possible T/N/M-like stage components) are negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: feature_067 Pearson r=-0.003, p=0.486. Mean pfs_months is essentially flat across Gleason 6-10 (3.71/3.76/3.74/3.74/3.70). Surprising null main effect for a Gleason-like variable.
All five 0-4 ordinal features are independent of pfs_months (|r|<0.01, all p>0.20). They do not behave like prognostic stage variables here.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS, feature_078, log(feature_013), feature_009, feature_006, and feature_057 each remain independent predictors of pfs_months.
feature_051 (univariate effect -0.52 mo) becomes non-significant after adjusting for the core covariates above.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078 + log(feature_013) + feature_009 + feature_006 + feature_051 + feature_057
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients: feature_078 +0.1737 (p≈0), log(feature_013) -0.349 (p≈0), feature_009 +0.478 (p≈0), feature_006 -0.077 (p≈0), feature_057 -1.150 (p≈0); feature_051 -0.002 (p=0.65, NS). R²=0.9689. feature_051 is fully confounded by the core predictors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the core covariates, race (feature_018) and insurance (feature_045) have no independent effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: All race coefficients |β|<0.003 with p>0.66; all insurance coefficients |β|<0.0005 with p>0.88. No adjusted disparity in pfs_months by race or insurance. R²=0.9689 (no improvement over base).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features, after adjusting for the five core continuous/ordinal covariates, at least one binary feature remains a strong independent predictor of pfs_months — the most likely candidates are treatment indicators with prevalences in the 0.05-0.50 range.

analyses.code: for b in binary_features: OLS pfs_months ~ core + b; rank by p
analyses.result_summary: Two binary features stand out after adjustment: feature_109 (prev 10.0%, β=+0.152, p=6.6e-181) and feature_039 (prev 10.2%, β=+0.149, p=6.2e-176). Smaller adjusted effects: feature_027 (60% prev, β=+0.025, p=7e-15), feature_043 (20%, β=-0.017, p=2e-5).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 and feature_039 have a strong positive interaction: the ~+0.15 month adjusted main effect of each is driven entirely by patients in whom both are present.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ core + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_109:feature_039
analyses.result_summary: Main effects collapse to ~0 once interaction is added: feature_109 alone β=-0.00005 (p=0.99); feature_039 alone β=-0.0004 (p=0.93); feature_109×feature_039 β=+1.437, p≈0. Crosstab: only 529 patients (1.06%) have both. The two features behave as a combination regimen.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combo (feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1) confers a +1.44 month adjusted increase in pfs_months versus all other patients.

analyses.result_summary: Combo prevalence 1.06% (n=529). Adjusted coefficient β=+1.4368 mo (p≈0). Unadjusted means: combo 5.22 mo vs non-combo 3.74 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combo treatment effect is significantly modified by Gleason score (feature_067): the benefit is concentrated in low-Gleason (6-7) patients and is essentially absent in high-Gleason (8-10) patients.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ core + _combo + lowG + _combo:lowG, where lowG = (feature_067<=7)
analyses.result_summary: Stratum-specific unadjusted combo effects: G=6 +2.79 mo (p=3e-26), G=7 +2.81 mo (p=2e-87), G=8 +0.06 (NS), G=9 +0.38 (NS), G=10 +0.07 (NS). Formal interaction: combo β=+0.021 (p=0.26) in high-G; combo×lowG β=+2.795 (p≈0). Combo benefit is essentially restricted to low Gleason.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among low-Gleason patients (feature_067 ≤ 7), the combo treatment yields a large absolute pfs_months gain (~+2.8 mo).
Among high-Gleason patients (feature_067 ≥ 8), the combo treatment yields essentially no pfs_months benefit.
Receipt of the combo (feature_109=1 AND feature_039=1) differs by race.
The combo treatment effect on pfs_months differs by race (treatment-effect heterogeneity).

analyses.result_summary: Combo prevalence by race in low-Gleason: asian 1.27%, black 1.08%, hispanic 0.97%, other 0.99%, white 1.06%. Chi²=0.96, p=0.916. No race-based disparity in combo receipt.

Combo×race interactions: Black β=-0.076 (p=0.10, NS), Hispanic β=-0.042 (p=0.31, NS), Asian β=+0.180 (p=0.004), Other β=+0.517 (p<0.0001). Asian/Other show enhanced combo effect; Black/Hispanic do not differ significantly from the white reference. No evidence that minority race patients respond worse on average; suggestive evidence that some groups respond better.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of the combo differs by insurance.
The combo treatment effect on pfs_months differs by insurance.

analyses.result_summary: Combo prevalence in low-Gleason by insurance: medicaid 1.11%, medicare 1.09%, private 0.99%, uninsured 1.33%. Chi²=1.35, p=0.718. No insurance-based disparity in combo receipt.

Combo×insurance interactions vs private: Medicare β=+0.149 (p<0.0001), Medicaid β=+0.264 (p<0.0001), Uninsured β=+0.234 (p=0.0009). All non-private insurance groups show stronger combo benefit than privately insured (counterintuitive direction; not consistent with disadvantage to public-insurance patients).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combo treatment effect is modified by feature_057 (3-level metastasis-like): it is largest in feature_057=0 and smallest in feature_057=2.

analyses.result_summary: Stratum-specific combo effects: feature_057=0 → +1.70 mo (p=3e-33), =1 → +1.34 mo (p=1e-30), =2 → +1.64 mo (p=5e-18). Adjusted interaction coef = -0.006, p=0.76 — i.e., NO significant interaction with feature_057 once core covariates are in. The combo effect is broadly consistent across metastasis levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combo treatment effect is attenuated in patients with higher log(feature_013) (PSA-like).
The combo treatment effect is attenuated in patients with higher feature_078.

analyses.result_summary: Combo × log(feature_013) interaction: β=-0.109, p=4.5e-24. Combo benefit shrinks ~0.11 mo per unit increase in log(PSA-like).
Combo × feature_078 interaction: β=-0.007 per unit, p=3.2e-7. Modest attenuation of combo benefit at higher feature_078 levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The prognostic effects of feature_078, log(feature_013), and feature_009 are themselves modified by feature_057 (stage-like): each prognostic factor's slope is smaller in higher feature_057 strata.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ core + (feature_X * feature_057) for X in {078, log13, 009}
analyses.result_summary: feature_078 × feature_057 β=-0.0097 (p≈0): feature_078 slope drops from ~0.18 at f057=0 to ~0.16 at f057=2. log(feature_013) × feature_057 β=+0.0205 (p=4.5e-32): negative log-PSA effect attenuates as f057 rises. feature_009 × feature_057 β=-0.0258 (p=3.2e-8): albumin-like benefit shrinks at higher metastasis level. All three interactions consistent with diminishing returns of favorable factors as disease burden increases.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 (binary, 60% prevalence) has a small but real positive adjusted effect on pfs_months.
feature_043 (binary, 20% prevalence) has a small but real negative adjusted effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: feature_027 adjusted β=+0.0238 mo (p=1.2e-15). Small but highly significant positive effect.

feature_043 adjusted β=-0.0156 mo (p=1.8e-5). Small but significant negative effect. Prevalence is balanced across race (19.5-20.1%) and insurance (19.7-21.6%), so this is unlikely to reflect a sociodemographic confounder.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within combo-treated low-Gleason patients, mean pfs_months does not differ by race.
Within combo-treated low-Gleason patients, mean pfs_months does not differ by insurance.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months in combo-treated low-Gleason (n=268): asian 6.20 (n=19), black 6.19 (n=33), hispanic 6.51 (n=37), other 6.02 (n=5), white 6.64 (n=174). Sample sizes per minority group are small but means are similar; no meaningful directional disadvantage.
Mean pfs_months in combo-treated low-Gleason: medicaid 6.72 (n=40), medicare 6.32 (n=116), private 6.76 (n=99), uninsured 5.96 (n=13). Uninsured slightly lower but n=13; otherwise similar.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious final model with the five core covariates plus the combo×low_Gleason interaction explains the vast majority of variance in pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078 + log(feature_013) + feature_009 + feature_006 + feature_057 + _combo + _lowg + _combo:_lowg
analyses.result_summary: Final model R²=0.9792. Coefficients (all p<<0.001 except as noted): feature_078 +0.1737, log(feature_013) -0.349, feature_009 +0.479, feature_006 -0.077, feature_057 -1.151, combo +0.021 (p=0.26 in high-G), low_Gleason -0.001 (p=0.56), combo×low_Gleason +2.795 (p≈0). The combo effect is fully captured by the low-Gleason subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Synthesis: the principal drivers of pfs_months in this cohort are five baseline biological factors (feature_078 ↑, log(feature_013) ↓, feature_009 ↑, feature_006 ↓, feature_057 ↓), one combination-treatment indicator (feature_109 AND feature_039) that benefits only low-Gleason patients (feature_067 ≤ 7), and small adverse/protective effects of feature_043 / feature_027. There is no marginal or adjusted disparity by race or insurance — neither in outcomes nor in receipt of the effective combination treatment.

analyses.code: Recap of all multivariable models built in iters 10-24
analyses.result_summary: All hypothesis components confirmed by the preceding iterations. Final-model R²=0.9792 with seven terms; race/insurance never enter as significant predictors of pfs_months at any modeling stage; combo treatment access and response are both equitable across race and insurance groups; clinical effect modification operates through Gleason-like grade (feature_067) and to a lesser extent log(feature_013) and feature_078, not through demographic strata.
